spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the headline capability the spec promises: navigator.storage.estimate() returns identical quota in normal and incognito. Aligned with the chromestatus motivation.

interactive.rationale: Real controls drive the demo (buttons, selects/inputs, live script) — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb on the feature index closely matches what the concept page delivers — title, lede, and interactive surface line up.

edge_cases.rationale: The simulator now compares the quota signal with separate simulated FileSystem API, service-worker cache eviction, and IndexedDB throughput side channels, clearly labeling them as outside the targeted quota fix.

references.rationale: The concept page now includes a see-also section with the canonical chromestatus.com/feature/5143175657291776 link.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex creeping in, button colours and contrast appear within AA.

summary: DevTools MCP verified /v151/incognitostaticstoragequota/privacy-flow-simulator/: moving the disk and threshold controls updated the decision trace, Chrome 151 Incognito stayed at the usage + 10 GiB bucket, the other-signal cards listed FileSystem API access, service-worker cache eviction speed, and IndexedDB transactions/sec, the ChromeStatus link was present, and the console had no errors.
